title: Suki's Sandwich Shop
synopsis: When Suki's family sandwich shop runs out of ingredients, she and her diverse group of friends work together to save the day by creating the most amazing sandwich the neighborhood has ever seen.

pages.text: Suki loved her family's sandwich shop. Every morning, she helped her mama slice tomatoes. She helped her papa stack bread. The shop smelled like fresh pickles and warm soup. "Today will be busy," said Mama. "The whole neighborhood comes on Saturday." Suki smiled. She loved busy days.

illustration.description: A cozy sandwich shop with warm yellow walls and red checkered floors. Suki, a young Asian girl with pigtails and a bright green apron, stands next to her mother who has kind eyes and wears a blue chef's hat. Fresh ingredients are scattered on wooden counters, and sunlight streams through the window. The mood is cheerful and inviting.
illustration.alt: Suki and her mother preparing ingredients in their family sandwich shop

pages.text: But when Suki opened the big refrigerator, her eyes grew wide. "Mama! Papa! Come quick!" she called. The shelves were almost empty. No cheese. No meat. Only three sad tomatoes sat in the corner. Papa scratched his head. "The delivery truck broke down," he said. "What will we tell our customers?"

illustration.description: Suki standing in front of an almost empty refrigerator with her mouth open in surprise. Her father, a tall man with glasses and a mustache, looks worried behind her. The refrigerator is mostly empty except for three lonely tomatoes on a shelf. The lighting is dramatic to show their concern.
illustration.alt: Suki discovering the empty refrigerator in the sandwich shop

pages.text: Just then, the door chimed. Mrs. Garcia walked in with her twin boys, Carlos and Diego. Behind them came Mr. Johnson with his daughter Zara. "We're here for sandwiches!" said Carlos. "Big ones!" added Diego. Suki looked at her parents. They looked worried. What would they do now?

illustration.description: The sandwich shop door opening with several diverse families entering. Mrs. Garcia, a Latina woman with curly black hair, walks in with two energetic boys. Mr. Johnson, a tall Black man with a warm smile, holds hands with his daughter Zara who has beautiful braided hair. Suki looks nervous in the foreground.
illustration.alt: Customers arriving at the sandwich shop while Suki looks worried

pages.text: Suki thought hard. Then her face lit up like sunshine. "I have an idea!" she announced. "What if everyone brings something from home? We can make one giant sandwich together!" Carlos jumped up and down. "I love giant things!" Diego clapped his hands. "Me too!" Even grown-ups started to smile.

illustration.description: Suki in the center of the shop with her finger raised and a bright, excited expression on her face. Around her, children are jumping and clapping with joy. The adults are starting to smile, and there's a sense of excitement building in the warm, cozy shop atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Suki announcing her idea to make a giant sandwich together

pages.text: "But wait," said Zara. "How big should this sandwich be?" Suki grinned. She spread her arms as wide as they could go. "THIS big!" she said. Everyone laughed. "That's really big!" said Mrs. Garcia. "We'll need lots of help." Mama and Papa looked at each other and nodded. "Let's try it," said Papa.

illustration.description: Suki with her arms stretched out as wide as possible, showing the enormous size of the sandwich. Everyone in the shop is laughing and smiling. Zara, a young Black girl with braided hair, looks amazed. The mood is joyful and full of anticipation.
illustration.alt: Suki demonstrating how big the sandwich should be with outstretched arms

pages.text: Everyone scattered like busy bees. Mrs. Garcia ran home for lettuce from her garden. Mr. Johnson brought back cheese from his refrigerator. Carlos and Diego returned with pickles. Zara carried a basket of fresh rolls from the bakery next door. "This is like a treasure hunt!" she said.

illustration.description: A montage-style illustration showing different people rushing around with ingredients. Mrs. Garcia carries bright green lettuce, Mr. Johnson holds yellow cheese, the twins have jars of pickles, and Zara has a wicker basket full of golden rolls. Everyone looks excited and determined.
illustration.alt: Everyone gathering ingredients from around the neighborhood

pages.text: Soon, more neighbors joined in. Ms. Kim brought sliced turkey. The Murphy family carried tomatoes from their roof garden. Old Mr. Peterson shuffled in with homemade mustard. "Been saving this for something special," he winked. The sandwich shop buzzed with happy voices and busy hands.

illustration.description: The sandwich shop now crowded with diverse neighbors of all ages bringing ingredients. Ms. Kim, an elderly Korean woman, holds sliced turkey. The Murphy family, a mixed-race family with red hair, carries red tomatoes. Mr. Peterson, an elderly white man with a cane, holds a jar of mustard with a mischievous wink.
illustration.alt: More neighbors arriving with various sandwich ingredients

pages.text: Suki looked around at all the food. Her heart felt warm and full. "We have everything we need!" she announced. "But how do we build a sandwich this big?" asked Carlos. Suki pointed to the long counter that ran across the whole shop. "Right there," she said. "It's perfect!"

illustration.description: Suki standing proudly in the middle of the shop surrounded by piles of colorful ingredients on every surface. She points toward a long wooden counter that stretches across the shop. Her expression is confident and happy, with ingredients creating a rainbow of colors around her.
illustration.alt: Suki pointing to the long counter where they'll build the giant sandwich

pages.text: Everyone worked together like a happy team. Papa sliced the enormous roll lengthwise. Mama spread mustard with a paint brush. "This is the biggest sandwich I've ever seen!" laughed Zara. Carlos and Diego arranged pickles in neat rows. "Don't eat them yet," warned Mrs. Garcia with a smile.

illustration.description: A busy scene showing everyone working together on the giant sandwich. Papa uses a large knife on an enormous roll, Mama wields a paint brush with mustard, and the children carefully arrange pickles. Everyone has focused, happy expressions as they work as a team.
illustration.alt: Everyone working together to build the giant sandwich

pages.text: Layer by layer, the sandwich grew taller. Turkey, cheese, lettuce, tomatoes, and more cheese. "It looks like a sandwich mountain!" said Diego. "A delicious mountain," added Carlos. Suki stood on her tiptoes to put the top piece of bread on. "Perfect!" she declared. Everyone cheered.

illustration.description: The completed giant sandwich towering like a small mountain on the counter, with layers of colorful ingredients visible from the side. Suki stands on her tiptoes placing the top piece of bread while everyone around her cheers with raised hands. The sandwich is impressively tall and looks delicious.
illustration.alt: The completed giant sandwich with everyone cheering around it

pages.text: "Now comes the best part," said Suki. "Eating it!" Papa cut the giant sandwich into pieces for everyone. Each piece was still huge. "I can barely fit this in my mouth!" giggled Zara. "That's what makes it fun!" said Suki. The whole shop filled with happy munching sounds.

illustration.description: Everyone sitting around tables in the shop taking enormous bites of their sandwich pieces. Zara's eyes are wide as she tries to fit her piece in her mouth, while others have chipmunk cheeks from big bites. The atmosphere is joyful and everyone looks delighted with their food.
illustration.alt: Everyone enjoying their pieces of the giant sandwich together

pages.text: "This is the best sandwich ever," said Mr. Johnson. "Because we made it together," added Mrs. Garcia. Mama and Papa smiled at Suki. "You saved the day with your big idea," said Mama. Suki looked around at all her friends and neighbors. Her heart felt as full as her tummy.

illustration.description: A warm group scene with everyone sitting together, satisfied and happy. Suki sits in the center looking content, surrounded by all the diverse neighbors who helped. The lighting is golden and warm, creating a cozy family atmosphere. Everyone has peaceful, satisfied expressions.
illustration.alt: Everyone sitting together contentedly after sharing the giant sandwich

pages.text: "Can we do this again next Saturday?" asked Carlos. "Even when the delivery truck works?" added Diego. Everyone laughed and nodded. "The Giant Sandwich Saturday!" announced Suki. "I like the sound of that," said Papa. And from that day on, Saturday became the most special day at Suki's family sandwich shop.

illustration.description: The shop from outside at sunset, with warm light glowing through the windows. A new sign in the window reads 'Giant Sandwich Saturday!' Silhouettes of all the friends can be seen inside, still talking and laughing together. The scene conveys warmth, community, and the promise of future adventures.
illustration.alt: The sandwich shop at sunset with everyone still inside celebrating their new tradition

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What problem did Suki's family face at the sandwich shop?
questions.explanation: The delivery truck broke down, so they had almost no ingredients left in the refrigerator to make sandwiches.

questions.question: What was Suki's big idea to solve the problem?
choices: Close the shop for the day
Ask everyone to bring ingredients from home
questions.explanation: Suki suggested that everyone could bring something from home to make one giant sandwich together.

questions.question: Where did they build the giant sandwich?
questions.explanation: Suki pointed to the long counter that ran across the whole shop as the perfect place to build the giant sandwich.

questions.question: What did they decide to call their new tradition?
questions.explanation: Suki announced 'Giant Sandwich Saturday' as the name for their new weekly tradition.

questions.question: Why was the sandwich so special according to Mrs. Garcia?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Garcia said it was the best sandwich ever because they made it together, emphasizing the importance of community cooperation.

pages.title: Complete the Sandwich Story
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words to complete Suki's story!
pages.text: Suki loved her family's _____ shop. When they ran out of ingredients, she had a big _____. Everyone brought something from _____ to make one giant sandwich _____. They built it on the long _____ and everyone _____!

pages.title: What Did Everyone Bring?
pages.instructions: Match the characters with what they brought for the giant sandwich!
pages.text: Mrs. Garcia brought _____ from her garden. Carlos and Diego brought _____. Zara carried _____ from the bakery. Mr. Peterson brought homemade _____.
